meta.author: Miguel Guhlin
meta.description: A curated portfolio of AI-powered tools, reference guides, coaching frameworks, and interactive resources built for educators, administrators, and knowledge workers.

projects.title: AI Capability Ladder
projects.subtitle: Self-assessment quiz for AI fluency in workplace contexts
projects.description: A five-question interactive self-assessment that scores your AI fluency and places you on the Capability Ladder — from Disconnected through Foundational, Integrated, to Strategic. Automatically logs responses to a data endpoint. Results include a full reference grid of what each level looks like in practice.

projects.title: AI Custom Instructions Library
projects.subtitle: Purpose-built AI assistants for educators & administrators
projects.description: A browsable library of copy-paste custom instructions deployable in any major Gen AI platform — ChatGPT, Claude Projects, Google Gems, BoodleBox, Copilot, or NotebookLM. Eight organized categories spanning productivity, communications, education, analysis, classroom bots, and specialty tools. Grew from the TCEA session on Automating Office Tasks with AI (March 2026).

projects.title: AI Prompt Architect
projects.subtitle: Transform basic ideas into engineered prompts; identify high-value AI workflows
projects.description: An interactive prompt optimizer that takes any basic request and transforms it using one of six evidence-backed frameworks: Triple-Check, Few-Shot, Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, Prompt Chaining, and RAG. Each framework includes a recommended model, a ready-to-copy optimized prompt, and rationale. Includes a glossary and two strategic workflow models (Problem-Based Learning and Use Case) for identifying high-impact AI opportunities.

projects.title: TCEA AI Skills Suite
projects.subtitle: Seven Claude-powered skills for mission-driven organizations
projects.description: Production-ready Claude skills (downloadable .skill files) for nonprofits and education-adjacent organizations. Each skill is a precision-engineered instruction set — install once in a Claude Project and Claude applies it to every matching request automatically. Includes an 8-step Cowork setup guide and the "Ask First" method for dramatically better outputs from the start.

projects.title: Coaching for Every Learner
projects.subtitle: Three UDL coaching protocols for instructional coaches and teachers
projects.description: An interactive scenario-based coaching tool grounded in CAST UDL Guidelines 3.0. Coaches and teachers work through real classroom challenges using three structured frameworks: ARC (Acknowledge–Reframe–Commit), Notice-Wonder-Next, and VIVA (Verify–Illustrate–Validate–Apply). Each protocol includes randomized scenario cards drawn from genuine instructional challenges, with UDL element alignment displayed for every response.

projects.title: Onboarding Guide Generator
projects.subtitle: One prompt → a complete branded HTML employee onboarding site
projects.description: A Claude Project system prompt that turns a company name into a complete, single-file HTML onboarding website. Claude searches the web for brand colors, locations, hours, and org structure before writing a line of HTML. The output includes a sticky header, sidebar table of contents, eight structured content sections, a first-week checklist, and amber-highlighted placeholders for any data Claude could not verify. Ships with two live examples: Mac Haik Georgetown (automotive) and TCEA.

projects.title: Client Workspace
projects.subtitle: Meeting notes, decisions, and next steps with session persistence
projects.description: A single-file HTML workspace for client and coaching sessions. Organizes meeting notes, key decisions, and next steps in a fixed sidebar layout with localStorage persistence across sessions. Includes Google Sheets sync via Apps Script for team-accessible record-keeping, client-side password protection, and a collapsible sidebar for focused writing mode. Designed for consultants, coaches, and PD facilitators who work with multiple clients.

projects.title: Think with Claude
projects.subtitle: Technical orientation screencasts for educators new to Claude
projects.description: A TCEA Knowledge Booster orientation series — four screencasts that get K–12 educators confident with Claude's interface before the deeper series begins. Covers account setup and navigation, Projects and file uploads, personalizing Claude with custom instructions, model selection, mobile app, and a full set of power habits including Julian date naming, session summaries, the Deep Re-Prompt method, and role assignment strategies.

projects.title: BoodleBox Basics & Beyond
projects.subtitle: AI office automation guide for the Texas Counseling Association
projects.description: A dual-purpose reference: a structured 6-part webinar guide on BoodleBox for educators, and a comprehensive office automation guide mapping TxCA administrative tasks to AI workflows. The automation guide covers 40+ tasks across seven categories (Member Communication, Events, Advocacy, Membership, CE Administration, Marketing, and Finance), each with approach type (Single Prompt vs. RAG), automation methods, and eight fully specified RAG configurations with system prompts.

projects.title: NSPA AI Training Series
projects.subtitle: All session web resources in one place — 4 sessions, 13 resources
projects.description: A consolidated resource index for the NSPA AI Training Series — four sessions aggregated into a single navigable page. Each session entry links directly to its slide deck, handouts, AI tool demos, and reference pages. Supplementary resources include an AI Fluency Levels reference, example workflow prompts, and the NSPA3 222 Framework. Designed for conference participants who need fast access to materials during or after sessions.

projects.title: Leadership Transition Toolkit
projects.subtitle: An Empedoclean framework for navigating nonprofit organizational change
projects.description: An interactive leadership reference grounded in Empedocles' four classical elements — Earth, Water, Fire, and Air — mapped to nonprofit organizational phases: Stability, Flow, Transformation, and Vision. Helps nonprofit leaders and boards diagnose where they are in a transition cycle and identify high-leverage actions. Includes reflection prompts, case examples, and a self-assessment keyed to each elemental phase.

projects.title: AI Literacy Roadmap
projects.subtitle: A phased framework for building durable AI competency in nonprofits
projects.description: A phased strategic framework for building durable AI competency in nonprofit organizations, structured across two parallel tracks: Personal Competency (individual staff) and Organizational Readiness (leadership and systems). Five sequential phases move from leadership alignment through tool adoption, team capability building, process redesign, and strategic integration. Each phase includes diagnostic questions, milestones, and common failure modes.

projects.title: Career Longevity in EdTech
projects.subtitle: Individual analysis vs. industry benchmarks — 1994 to present
projects.description: An interactive data visualization comparing one EdTech leader's career tenure data against national benchmarks for K–12, nonprofit, and public sector positions. Features an animated career timeline, comparative bar charts, and four key takeaways. Built with scroll-triggered animations and a fixed navigation dot system — demonstrates what AI-assisted data storytelling looks like when applied to personal career data rather than abstract organizational metrics.

projects.title: Visible Learning with Ed Tech
projects.subtitle: Evidence-based teaching strategies, SOLO Taxonomy, and AI guidance
projects.description: A comprehensive strategy reference grounded in John Hattie's Visible Learning research, Mike Bell's memory science, and CAST's UDL 3.0 Guidelines. Covers the three phases of learning (Surface, Deep, Transfer), the SOLO Taxonomy with detailed level descriptors, a sortable strategy table with effect sizes, and deep dives into Jigsaw (ES: 0.92), Reciprocal Teaching (ES: 0.74), Concept Mapping (ES: 0.66), and Transfer Learning. Includes six instructional frameworks and a SOLO-mapped guide to responsible AI use at each level.

projects.title: Fun Personality Quizzes
projects.subtitle: Which character matches your teaching style? Four themed educator quizzes
projects.description: Four five-question personality quizzes that help educators discover which iconic character best matches their teaching style. Each quiz is fully self-contained, mobile-friendly, and requires no login. Themes span Winnie the Pooh, The Smurfs, Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs, and Looney Tunes. Designed as a low-stakes icebreaker for PD sessions, team meetings, or classroom community building.

projects.title: TCEA AI Tools Hub
projects.subtitle: Custom instructions library, KPI dashboard, and instructional strategy references
projects.description: A TCEA-branded hub of interrelated reference tools built for district and campus leaders. The index is the TCEA edition of the AI Custom Instructions Library. Supporting tools include a KPI dashboard for school data visualization, a Surface-Deep-Transfer learning reference, a top instructional strategies guide, and an AI news digest. Companion to the mguhlin.github.io/aici personal edition.

projects.title: Media Processing Tools
projects.subtitle: Drag-and-drop batch scripts for Windows media conversion and optimization
projects.description: Eight Windows batch scripts for drag-and-drop media conversion and document processing using FFmpeg, ImageMagick, and Pandoc. Covers audio-to-MP3 extraction, bulk video conversion, audio noise fix, PNG compression, media-to-image conversion, PNG shrinking, WebM encoding, and universal text format conversion. No GUI required — drop a file on the script and it runs.

projects.title: Linux Media Tools
projects.subtitle: Bash scripts for audio, video, image, and document processing on Linux
projects.description: Six Bash scripts for Linux that mirror the Windows Media Tools suite — built for Pop!_OS and Ubuntu. Uses FFmpeg for audio extraction and video encoding, ImageMagick for image compression and resizing, and Pandoc for document format conversion. Scripts are designed for terminal use or integration into file manager right-click actions. Recommended over the Windows versions for any Linux-primary workflow.
